card.intuition: Precompute cumulative sums so any range query [l, r] costs O(1) instead of O(n).
card.analogy: A bank statement with a running balance — to find how much you spent in any month range, subtract the opening balance of that range from the closing balance. You don't re-add every transaction.
card.time_complexity: O(n) build, O(1) per range query
when_to_use: Multiple range sum queries on a static array
Counting subarrays with a given sum (prefix_sum[r] - prefix_sum[l-1] = target)
2D prefix sums for rectangular region queries in a matrix
Difference arrays for range update + point query (inverse of prefix sum)
gotchas: Use a 1-indexed prefix array of size n+1 (prefix[0] = 0) to avoid off-by-one errors in range queries
prefix[r] - prefix[l-1] gives sum of arr[l..r] — the fence-post is l-1, not l
Prefix sums only work for static arrays — use a Fenwick tree if you need point updates too

exercise.prompt: Build a prefix sum array and implement an O(1) range sum query.
exercise.solution:
prefix[i + 1] = prefix[i] + arr[i]
return prefix[hi + 1] - prefix[lo]
hints: Each prefix entry is the previous entry plus the current array value: prefix[i+1] = prefix[i] + arr[i]
Range sum arr[lo..hi] = total up to hi+1 minus total up to lo: prefix[hi+1] - prefix[lo]
exercise.test_code:
arr = [3, 1, 4, 1, 5, 9, 2, 6]
pre = build_prefix(arr)
assert len(pre) == len(arr) + 1,          'Prefix array must have length n+1'
assert pre[0] == 0,                        'Sentinel: prefix[0] must be 0'
assert range_sum(pre, 0, 0) == 3,          'Single element: arr[0]=3'
assert range_sum(pre, 0, 7) == sum(arr),   'Full range must equal sum(arr)'
assert range_sum(pre, 2, 5) == 4+1+5+9,   'arr[2..5] = 4+1+5+9 = 19'
assert range_sum(pre, 4, 4) == 5,          'arr[4..4] = 5'
print('PASS')
